title: NO SPOILERS Evangelion 30 Speculation 2
wikitext:
[]: [NO SPOILERS] Evangelion 3.0 Speculation [2] Post by Reichu » Sat Oct 27, 2012 11:35 am Continued from here: Brainman wrote: It wouldn't be so much about believing if Shinji could hurt her. The inner beef there would be the notion of "He saved wondergirl, but no one saved me." And like I said, it probably wont be Shinji she has this conflict with. It'll probably be either Misato or Mari.

[]: Post by Removal » Sat Oct 27, 2012 1:16 pm So, any thoughts on Angels in 3.0? I think the similarities between Gaghiel and the new Third Angel in 2.0 could have interesting connotations for Angel appearances in the new film (yes, I believe there is a purposeful connection between those two).

[]: Post by Reichu » Sat Oct 27, 2012 5:31 pm Removal wrote: So, any thoughts on Angels in 3.0?

[]: Post by Monk Ed » Sat Oct 27, 2012 6:09 pm That thing I said earlier about Kaworu being the mastermind behind the Angels might have been a joke, but it's gotten me seriously considering that Kaworu might, one way or another, be outright malevolent in NME. I so don't want that to happen :( which makes it feel even more likely that it will.

[]: Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sat Oct 27, 2012 6:35 pm Monk Ed wrote: That thing I said earlier about Kaworu being the mastermind behind the Angels might have been a joke, but it's gotten me seriously considering that Kaworu might, one way or another, be outright malevolent in NME.

[]: Post by driftking18594 » Sun Oct 28, 2012 8:48 am Monk Ed wrote: That thing I said earlier about Kaworu being the mastermind behind the Angels might have been a joke, but it's gotten me seriously considering that Kaworu might, one way or another, be outright malevolent in NME.

[]: Post by Lurkis » Sun Oct 28, 2012 11:33 am Monk Ed wrote: That thing I said earlier about Kaworu being the mastermind behind the Angels might have been a joke, but it's gotten me seriously considering that Kaworu might, one way or another, be outright malevolent in NME. I so don't want that to happen :( which makes it feel even more likely that it will.

[]: Post by robersora » Sun Oct 28, 2012 12:14 pm ^ Well, pretty much nothing... As you can see in EoE.

[]: Post by Lurkis » Sun Oct 28, 2012 1:33 pm robersora wrote: ^ Well, pretty much nothing... As you can see in EoE.

[]: Post by robersora » Sun Oct 28, 2012 2:32 pm I have that feeling, that ShinjixKaworu-time will be splashed over the whole movie, with Kaworu dying at the end. This won't be that interesting... Interesting will be what Anno intends to do with Mari and Asuka... I think Mari works outside of words like benevolent and malevolent. She is just outright crazy and will do everything to reach her goal.

[]: Post by Brainman » Sun Oct 28, 2012 2:34 pm Lurkis wrote: On another note, anyone think Mari is a little malevolent?

[]: Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sun Oct 28, 2012 2:46 pm From what she said in the opening scene of 2.0 she seems well-versed at using adults to get what she wants. I find it hard to get emotionally attached to anyone in rebuild.

[]: Post by riffraff11235 » Sun Oct 28, 2012 2:56 pm bobbyfischer's ghost wrote: From what she said in the opening scene of 2.0 she seems well-versed at using adults to get what she wants.

[]: Post by StratoSakuya...AF » Sun Oct 28, 2012 6:06 pm Driftking18594 wrote: IIRC someone here called Kaworu the God of the Evaverse. Perhaps he may show that if the Abrahamic God was real, he would be an absolute dick.

[]: Post by Monk Ed » Sun Oct 28, 2012 6:42 pm StratoSakuya...AF wrote: That would be me. But the whole idea wasn't suggesting he was malevolent, but rather that he had a plan to change the world, whether for better or worse. Given what I've seen, it leans towards the former.

[]: Post by Lurkis » Mon Oct 29, 2012 7:02 am bobbyfischer's ghost wrote: I find it hard to get emotionally attached to anyone in rebuild.

[]: Post by Gus Hanson » Mon Oct 29, 2012 9:17 am I imagine that if Kaworu said "I love you" to Shinji, he'd say it in a different setting than the NERV bathhouse. Saying the most important feeling Shinji's ever had said to him would be better absorbed somewhere that doesn't involve him seeing Kaworu's junk.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Oct 29, 2012 10:30 am bobbyfischer's ghost wrote: From what she said in the opening scene of 2.0 she seems well-versed at using adults to get what she wants.

[]: Post by Hiten777 » Mon Oct 29, 2012 11:15 am Regarding the Angels in Rebuild, for sure from the previews shown this summer Arael is definitely in there, shown briefly in a fight with the newly repaired Unit 02. I said in another threat that I believe the Kaworu in rebuild is a combination of the anime and manga counterparts. In the anime, Kaworu is introduced as someone Shinji can call his friend and understand. In the manga, however, Kaworu is shown to have an innocent, yet callous, view of life, which causes Shinji to dislike or even loathe him in the beginning. He strangled a kitten to death since, by his reasoning, it would've died anyway from starvation. From the new trailer, I think Kaworu starts off as the kinda guy Shinji befriends and gets along with, until Kaworu starts showing his callous take on life and letting people die, causing Shinji to get angry and reject him.

[]: Post by riffraff11235 » Mon Oct 29, 2012 11:29 am Gus Hanson wrote: I imagine that if Kaworu said "I love you" to Shinji, he'd say it in a different setting than the NERV bathhouse. Saying the most important feeling Shinji's ever had said to him would be better absorbed somewhere that doesn't involve him seeing Kaworu's junk.

The inner beef there would be the notion of "He saved wondergirl, but no one saved me." And like I said, it probably wont be Shinji she has this conflict with.
